---
title: "Client Report - What's in a Name?"
subtitle: "Unit 1 Task 2"
author: "[Gavin Blair]"
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
    page-layout: full
    title-block-banner: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: body
    number-sections: false
    html-math-method: katex
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show the code"
    code-overflow: wrap
    code-copy: hover
    code-tools:
        source: false
        toggle: true
        caption: See code
execute: 
  warning: false
    
---

```{python}
import polars as pl
import numpy as np
from lets_plot import *
LetsPlot.setup_html(isolated_frame=True)
```

```{python}
# Learn morea about Code Cells: https://quarto.org/docs/reference/cells/cells-jupyter.html

# Include and execute your code here
df = pl.read_csv("https://posit.byui.edu/content/0d43d66f-d3b7-4c43-8c36-52e3ed69e7f1/names_year_csv.csv")
```


## QUESTION 1

<!-- Make sure at least one of your charts includes reference marks!! -->

__How does your name at your birth year compare to its use historically? Your must provide a chart. The years labels on your charts should not include a comma.__ 

_type your results and analysis here_

```{python}
# Q1
usa = df.filter(pl.col('name') == 'Gavin')

(
    ggplot(data=usa)
    + geom_line(aes(x='year', y='Total'))
    + scale_x_continuous(
        limits=[1900, 2020],
        breaks=list(range(1900, 2021, 10))
    )
    # reference line for your birth year
    + geom_vline(xintercept=2005, linetype='dashed', color='red')
    + geom_text(x=2006, y=usa['Total'].max(), label='2005 (My Birth Year)', color='red')
    + labs(
        title="Popularity of the Name 'Gavin' Over Time",
        x="Year",
        y="Number of Babies Named Gavin"
    )
)

print("My birth year is 2005, which was when the name 'Gavin satrted to gain some popularity. It hasn't reached its peak yet.")

```


## QUESTION 2

__Think of a unique name from a famous movie. Plot the usage of that name and see how changes line up with the movie release. Does it look like the movie had an effect on usage?__  

_type your results and analysis here_

```{python}
# Q2
usa = df.filter(pl.col('name') == 'Anakin')

(
    ggplot(data=usa)
    + geom_line(aes(x='year', y='Total'))
    + scale_x_continuous(
        limits=[1900, 2020],
        breaks=list(range(1900, 2021, 10))
    )
)

print("I know that 'Anakin' is an obvious choice, but I can't think of anything else. It should be obvious why the name got popular very quickly.")
```

## QUESTION 3

__Mary, Martha, Peter, and Paul are all Christian names. From 1920 - 2000, compare the name usage of each of the four names in a single chart. What trends do you notice?__  

_type your results and analysis here_

```{python}
# Q3
usa = df.filter(
    pl.col('name').is_in(['Mary', 'Martha', 'Peter', 'Paul'])
)
(
    ggplot(data=usa)
    + geom_line(aes(x='year', y='Total'))
    + scale_x_continuous(
        limits=[1920, 2000],
        breaks=list(range(1920, 2000, 10))
    )
)

print("I noticed that these names started in decline in popularity in the 1970's.")
```
<!-- Make sure at least one of your charts includes reference marks!! -->
